title: Episode 16 and The Sickness Unto Death
display_title: Episode 16 and The Sickness Unto Death
wikitext:
[]: Episode 16 and The Sickness Unto Death             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Tue Mar 08, 2011 1:16 am Episode 16 is named after Soren Kierkegaard's book, The Sickness Unto Death . I haven't read that book in a while (and when I did, I only understood certain parts of it), but I remember the statement from it "the self is the relationship to oneself." What happens in the episode to me seems to tie into that, with the "observed self" of Shinji meeting "the self that observes itself." So, do the connections to Kierkegaard's book run deeper than just the episode title? Discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Tue Mar 08, 2011 11:33 am Isn't the episode named "Splitting of the Breast"?

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Tue Mar 08, 2011 11:49 am A.T. Fish wrote: Isn't the episode named "Splitting of the Breast"?

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Tue Mar 08, 2011 4:21 pm Born of Lilith wrote: So, do the connections to Kierkegaard's book run deeper than just the episode title?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Mar 08, 2011 4:51 pm Platinum booklet commentaries has a little bit about it: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=15758#15758

What happens in the episode to me seems to tie into that, with the "observed self" of Shinji meeting "the self that observes itself." So, do the connections to Kierkegaard's book run deeper than just the episode title? Discuss.
